forward_patch:
--- original
+++ modified
@@ -0,0 +1,27 @@
+---
+depends_on:
+- 01KXX47408MG0KP7BAQWFKZFDW
+position_column: todo
+position_ordinal: '8780'
+title: Corpus migration golden tests (render â†’ include)
+---
+## What
+Pin the swissarmyhammer content carryover with golden tests (plan.md Â§4): the Liquid corpus renders through Stencil nearly verbatim; the single `{% render %}` becomes `{% include %}`.
+
+- Copy the *templated* subset of `../swissarmyhammer/builtin/` (files containing `{{ }}` or `{% %}` constructs, plus every partial they reference â€” the survey found `{% include %}` Ă—20, `{% if %}` Ă—3, `{% render %}` Ă—1, `{% for %}` Ă—1, zero filters) into `Tests/FoundationModelsExtrasTests/Fixtures/Corpus/` preserving relative layout (`_partials/`, `agents/`, `skills/`).
+- Apply the one-time migration: `{% render '_partials/validators' %}` in `_partials/coding-standards.md` becomes `{% include "_partials/validators" %}`. Nothing else changes.
+- Check in expected rendered outputs beside the fixtures (`Expected/` mirror tree), generated once and reviewed by hand â€” e.g. `available_skills` given a small fixed array so `{% if %}`/`{% for %}` take both branches across the corpus.
+- Golden test renders every corpus file through `TemplateEngine` (trusted, partials stack rooted at the corpus fixture) and diffs against the expected output byte-for-byte. A second pass renders the same corpus **untrusted** and asserts it passes validation â€” the corpus uses only whitelisted constructs.
+
+## Acceptance Criteria
+- [ ] Every templated corpus file renders without error and matches its checked-in expected output
+- [ ] The migrated `render`â†’`include` file renders identically to intent (validators partial inlined)
+- [ ] Corpus renders clean under `Trust.untrusted` as well
+- [ ] Fixture tree checked in; test discovers files by enumeration, not a hand-kept list
+
+## Tests
+- [ ] `Tests/FoundationModelsExtrasTests/CorpusGoldenTests.swift` implementing the above; failure output names the file and shows a diff
+- [ ] Run `swift test`; expect all pass
+
+## Workflow
+- Use `/tdd` â€” write failing tests first, then implement to make them pass.
\ No newline at end of file

 ## What
-Pin the swissarmyhammer content carryover with golden tests (plan.md Â§4): the Liquid corpus renders through Stencil nearly verbatim; the single `{% render %}` becomes `{% include %}`.
+Pin the swissarmyhammer content carryover with golden tests (plan.md Â§4): the Liquid corpus renders through Stencil nearly verbatim, with a small, explicit one-time migration.

-- Copy the *templated* subset of `../swissarmyhammer/builtin/` (files containing `{{ }}` or `{% %}` constructs, plus every partial they reference â€” the survey found `{% include %}` Ă—20, `{% if %}` Ă—3, `{% render %}` Ă—1, `{% for %}` Ă—1, zero filters) into `Tests/FoundationModelsExtrasTests/Fixtures/Corpus/` preserving relative layout (`_partials/`, `agents/`, `skills/`).
-- Apply the one-time migration: `{% render '_partials/validators' %}` in `_partials/coding-standards.md` becomes `{% include "_partials/validators" %}`. Nothing else changes.
-- Check in expected rendered outputs beside the fixtures (`Expected/` mirror tree), generated once and reviewed by hand â€” e.g. `available_skills` given a small fixed array so `{% if %}`/`{% for %}` take both branches across the corpus.
+- Copy the *templated* subset of `../swissarmyhammer/builtin/` (files containing `{{ }}` or `{% %}` constructs, plus every partial they reference) into `Tests/FoundationModelsExtrasTests/Fixtures/Corpus/` preserving relative layout â€” that includes `_partials/`, `agents/`, `skills/`, **and `validators/`** (~20 validator files use `{{version}}` in frontmatter).
+- One-time migration (plan.md's "nothing else changes" needs two qualifications found by corpus audit):
+  - `{% render '_partials/validators' %}` in `_partials/coding-standards.md` â†’ `{% include "_partials/validators" %}`
+  - `available_skills.size` in `_partials/skills.md` â†’ `available_skills.count` â€” `.size` is Liquid; Stencil resolves `.count` on collections, and `.size` would silently evaluate falsy
+- Special case: `validators/no-secrets/rules/no-secrets.md` contains a **literal** `` {{secret}} `` (documentation of a placeholder pattern, not a template variable). Stencil renders missing variables as empty string, silently corrupting that line. Either exclude the file from the golden suite or deliberately pin the eaten-placeholder output with a comment explaining why â€” decide and document in the test.
+- Golden context must exercise every branch: define `available_skills` (small fixed array), `version`, and `arguments` â€” and render `{% if %}` sites in both truthy and absent variants so both branches are pinned.
+- Check in expected rendered outputs beside the fixtures (`Expected/` mirror tree), generated once and reviewed by hand.
 - Golden test renders every corpus file through `TemplateEngine` (trusted, partials stack rooted at the corpus fixture) and diffs against the expected output byte-for-byte. A second pass renders the same corpus **untrusted** and asserts it passes validation â€” the corpus uses only whitelisted constructs.

 ## Acceptance Criteria
-- [ ] Every templated corpus file renders without error and matches its checked-in expected output
-- [ ] The migrated `render`â†’`include` file renders identically to intent (validators partial inlined)
+- [ ] Every templated corpus file (including `validators/`) renders without error and matches its checked-in expected output
+- [ ] The two migrated constructs (`render`â†’`include`, `.size`â†’`.count`) render to the intended output
+- [ ] `{% if %}` sites are pinned in both truthy and falsy context variants
 - [ ] Corpus renders clean under `Trust.untrusted` as well
 - [ ] Fixture tree checked in; test discovers files by enumeration, not a hand-kept list

forward_patch:
--- original
+++ modified
@@ -1,4 +1,30 @@
 ---
+comments:
+- actor: claude-code
+  id: 01kxxtqsb4z2sw3h9wfqfe9g5t
+  text: |-
+    Implemented CorpusGoldenTests.swift plus the fixture tree.
+
+    Corpus audit: grep for `{{`/`{%` across ../swissarmyhammer/builtin/ found 49 templated files; plus 7 plain partials those files transitively reference via include/render (architecture-awareness, task-standards, short-ids, record-progress, review-column, task-double-check, validators) = 56 total files copied preserving relative layout into Tests/FoundationModelsExtrasTests/Fixtures/Corpus/.
+
+    Migration applied directly to the copied fixtures:
+    - _partials/coding-standards.md: the one {% render %} became {% include %}
+    - _partials/skills.md: available_skills.size became available_skills.count
+
+    Verified via Stencil source (Variable.swift, Expression.swift, IfTag.swift, ForTag.swift) that an absent context key resolves to nil without throwing, and nil safely evaluates falsy in both {% if %} numeric/variable expressions and {% for %} (empty result) -- so the "absent" branch variant is implemented by simply omitting the available_skills/arguments keys, no special-casing needed.
+
+    Golden mirrors: Fixtures/Expected/ (populated context: fixed available_skills array + arguments + version) and Fixtures/ExpectedAbsent/ (absent: available_skills/arguments omitted, version still set) -- both full mirrors of the 56-file corpus, generated via a CORPUS_GOLDEN_UPDATE=1 env-var regeneration path in the test itself (reviewed by hand after generation, matching the "generated once and reviewed by hand" instruction).
+
+    no-secrets.md special case: decided to pin the eaten-placeholder output (not exclude the file) -- Stencil renders the literal `{{secret}}` prose example as empty, producing an empty backtick pair in the rendered "Configuration file placeholders" line. Documented with an inline comment and a dedicated test.
+
+    TDD: ran swift test --filter CorpusGoldenTests before generating any Expected/ExpectedAbsent content -- confirmed RED (112 issues: 56 files x 2 context variants, all "no checked-in expected output"). Generated once with CORPUS_GOLDEN_UPDATE=1, hand-reviewed the output (spot-checked coding-standards.md's nested-frontmatter carryover from the migrated include, skills.md's if/for both branches, ci/SKILL.md and map/SKILL.md's if-arguments both branches, no-secrets.md's eaten placeholder), then re-ran normally -- GREEN.
+
+    Side finding: adding Fixtures/ under the test target's own source path made SwiftPM emit a new "unhandled files" build warning (168 files). Fixed by declaring resources: [.copy("Fixtures")] on the FoundationModelsExtrasTests target in Package.swift -- confirmed via git stash that this warning did not exist before this change and is gone after the fix.
+
+    Full suite: swift test -> 103 tests, 12 suites, all passed, exit 0, zero warnings.
+
+    Adversarial double-check spawned to verify acceptance criteria against the actual fixtures/test code before handoff.
+  timestamp: 2026-07-19T18:38:10.788896+00:00
 depends_on:
 - 01KXX47408MG0KP7BAQWFKZFDW
 - 01KXX47J8F72GSX5STK6YVDQZP
